changes_summary:
Revised plan to address two significant flags:

**FLAG-001 (mute/detach eligibility):** Added volume-gated eligibility rule — waveforms only render on clips with `volume > 0`. After `detachAudioFromVideo` sets the visual clip to `volume: 0`, no waveform appears on it (correctly moves to the new audio track clip). This rule is threaded through Steps 3.4 and 4.1, and the detach-audio flow is added to Step 5 manual verification.

**FLAG-002 (video extraction fragility):** Made `decodeAudioData` failure handling explicit in Step 1.3 — if decode fails or returns silence, hook returns `null` gracefully. Demoted video-on-visual-track waveforms from `must` to `should` in success criteria since extraction depends on the video having an audio stream. Added MIME type gating (`assetEntry.type?.startsWith('video/')`) in Step 4.1 so we only attempt extraction on actual video files.

Other changes: removed assumption about showing waveforms on muted clips, added detach-audio flow to smoke test, clarified eligibility rules in a single location (Step 3.4) referenced by Step 4.
questions: Should video clips on visual tracks automatically show waveforms (attempting decodeAudioData on any video file), or should this be opt-in (e.g., only after user explicitly requests it)? Current plan: auto-show for all video clips with volume > 0, fail gracefully if no audio.
Is there a preferred color/style for the waveform? (e.g., should it match the clip's selection color, use a fixed color like the existing sky-blue accent, or vary by track kind?)
What is the typical row height in practice? If it's very small (< 24px), a mirrored waveform may be unreadable and a simple amplitude bar chart would be better.

success_criteria.criterion: Audio clips on audio tracks display a waveform visualization inside the clip rectangle on the timeline
Waveform accurately reflects the trimmed portion of the clip (respects from/to/speed)
Waveform does not interfere with existing clip interactions (click, drag, resize, context menu)
Waveform data is cached per audio URL to avoid redundant fetches/decoding
Existing video editor tests continue to pass
Text clips and effect layers do not show waveforms
Clips with volume: 0 (including post-detach visual clips) do not show waveforms
Video clips with embedded audio on visual tracks display a waveform when volume > 0 and decodeAudioData succeeds
Waveform updates bucket count when the timeline is zoomed (scale changes)
No visible jank when loading a timeline with 10+ audio clips
Waveform is visually readable at the default row height without obscuring clip labels
Manual smoke test: add audio clip, add video clip, detach audio, zoom in/out, trim clip, mute clip — all render correctly

assumptions: Row height is approximately 32-40px based on the ACTION_VERTICAL_MARGIN usage and typical timeline UIs — waveform design will be optimized for this range.
decodeAudioData works on video files (MP4, WebM) in modern browsers to extract the audio track — this is standard Web Audio API behavior, but may fail for videos without audio streams.
The getActionRender callback in TimelineEditor.tsx can be extended to pass additional props (audioSrc, clipWidth) without major refactoring.
Waveforms should NOT render on clips with volume: 0 — after audio detach, the visual clip is muted and showing a waveform would be misleading.
No new npm dependencies are needed — Web Audio API's decodeAudioData is sufficient for waveform extraction.
SVG rendering is performant enough for this use case (one path element per clip, no animation).
AssetRegistryEntry.type contains a MIME type string (e.g., 'video/mp4', 'audio/mpeg') that can be used to determine if a file is a video.
